plan.reasoning: Step 0: SMILES Consistency Check. SMILES is [H], representing atomic hydrogen, a radical with one unpaired electron. No heavy atoms, so the atom index list is []. All succeeded as perfect adsorptions, no failures listed. No [🔄 Converged to known best] tag, so not duplicate. Energies are close for the hollow sites (-3.134 and -3.135 eV), but geometrically possibly distinct as per history (different initial sites and slight energy difference). Major sites tested: ontop and hollow, but bridge sites (Na-Pb, Pb-Pb) not yet tested. Surface is heterogeneous (Na, Pb), so bridge sites may yield different energies. Not all major high-symmetry sites tested, and energies are close but no termination tag, so continue. Step 2: Formulate New Plan. Propose a new plan: bridge on Pb-Pb, as Pb-rich sites gave lower energies in previous tries. This is different from all prior plans. Step 3: User intent is to find lowest-energy adsorption of atomic H. Step 4: Adsorbate is atomic H (radical), no heavy atoms, binding via index 0. Step 5: Available bridge sites include Pb-Pb.
